title: Midea HVAC Serial Protocol Field Glossary Schema
description: Validates the structure of serial_glossary.yaml — the machine-readable field glossary for the Midea HVAC serial protocol (UART/R/T).

version.description: Semantic version of the glossary format.

protocol_focus.description: Primary protocol layer this glossary covers.

primary_bus.description: Buses with full field entries (uart, rt).

reference_buses.description: Buses referenced in notes only (xye, mainboard, ir).

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date of last content update.

encodings.description: Reusable encoding templates referenced by field protocol entries.

fields.description: Two top-level categories: control (settable) and sensor (read-only).

frames.description: Machine-readable query-frame specs. Each entry is a canonical frame ID that the planner uses to build and dedup queries. Sibling of 'fields' and 'encodings' — the single source of truth for what bytes hit the wire. See TODO_glossary.md §6.

protocol_generations.description: Protocol-key → generation map. Drives infer_generation() and the source-priority semantics in field_query.read_field(). Each value is 'legacy' (legacy family (protocol appendix §4.3) — C0, C1*, B1, 0x40), 'new' (B0/B5 family), or null (generation deliberately unknown — heartbeats and frames whose role is not yet decided). Keys absent from this dict resolve to null at lookup time. Edit this dict to add a new frame; no Python change is needed.

unit.description: Physical unit of the decoded value, e.g. '°C', 'kWh'.

scale.description: Resolution per raw unit, e.g. 0.5 means each raw step = 0.5 °C.

offset.description: Raw value corresponding to 0 in decoded units.

range.description: [min, max] of decoded value in physical units.

byte_count.description: Number of consecutive bytes this encoding reads from the body (default 1). The decoder reads body[offset..offset+byte_count].

byte_order.description: Byte order for generic multi-byte encodings (uint16_le, uint16_be, uint24_le, uint24_be). Dispatched in apply_encoding instead of name pattern matching.

frame_spec.description: Canonical query-frame specification. Referenced from the top-level 'frames' dict. The codec reads these entries via build_frame_from_spec() to produce wire bytes; the planner reads 'triggers' to match fields to frames. See TODO_glossary.md §6.

purpose.description: One-line human explanation of what this frame does.

msg_type.description: Application-layer message type byte (UART frame byte[9]). Default 0x03 (MEAS/query).

triggers.description: List of rsp_* frame keys this query causes the AC to emit. The planner uses this to match fields (which reference rsp_* keys in their protocols blocks) to the right query frame.

bus.description: Buses this frame is valid on. Omit for frames valid on both. The planner skips frames whose bus list doesn't include the current runtime bus. Some queries are bus-specific: C1 group queries with body[1]=0x81 only work on R/T (extension board); body[1]=0x21 variants only work on UART (WiFi dongle). See TODO_glossary.md §8.

sources.description: Evidence trail — capture sessions, community projects, code references.

oneOf.description: Literal body bytes, zero-padded to the declared length.

length.description: Final body length in bytes, after zero-padding.

bytes.description: Literal byte values starting at body[0]. Shorter than 'length' → padded with 0x00.

oneOf.description: Sparse byte placement — only the listed positions are set, the rest are zero.

bytes_at.description: Map of byte index (as string key) to value. Unspecified positions are zero-filled.

oneOf.description: Body assembled at call time by walking per-field 'decode' arrays whose protocol key matches 'assembled_from'. Used for set frames (cmd_0x40, cmd_0xb0) where the body content depends on the specific fields being written.

length.description: Optional — fixed body length if known (cmd_0x40 = 26). Omit for variable-length command bodies (cmd_0xb0).

assembled_from.description: The protocol key (e.g. 'cmd_0x40') whose per-field decode arrays describe where to pack each field's bits.

confidence_label.description: Per AGENTS.md: confirmed > consistent > hypothesis > disputed > unknown.

direction.description: Data flow direction: command (host→AC, modifies state), query (host→AC, triggers response without modifying state), response (AC→host), both, or capability (queried once).

data_type.description: Wire-level data type of the field.

device_context.description: Which physical device this field primarily relates to.

byte.description: Human-readable byte/bit position for documentation. Machine parsing uses 'decode' instead.

decode.description: Machine-parseable decode chain (priority order). First step whose condition is met wins. See decode_step for structure.

encoding.description: Key into the top-level encodings dict. Prefer decode[].encoding for new entries.

decode_step.description: A single decode step. Either offset+bits (extract from body) or logic+sources (combine multiple extractions).

property_id.description: B0/B1 TLV property ID as 'lo,hi' hex pair (e.g. '0x4B,0x00'). When present, the decoder first locates the TLV record with this property ID, then applies offset/bits relative to the record's data bytes (not the frame body). Used for property-protocol fields (cmd_0xb0 / rsp_0xb1). Mutually exclusive with logic/sources.

offset.description: Byte index. For fixed-body frames (C0, C1): body[N]. For property-addressed frames (B0/B1, when property_id is set): data byte N within the matched TLV record's payload.

encoding.description: Key into top-level encodings dict. Applied after bit extraction. Multi-byte encodings (BCD etc.) define byte_count in the encoding definition.

add.description: Constant added to the extracted value before encoding.

condition.description: Simple condition on extracted value: '!= 0', '> 0'. Step skipped if not met.

half_bit.description: Extra 0.5 flag from another bit position. Adds 0.5 if the specified bit is set.

logic.description: Combine sources with boolean logic. Mutually exclusive with offset/bits.

sources.description: Array of {offset, bits} when logic is set.

description.description: One-line human-readable description of what this field represents.

default_priority.description: Per-field default priority list consumed by field_query.read_field() when no explicit priority is passed. Each entry is a scope token: 'protocol_new', 'protocol_legacy', 'protocol_unknown', 'protocol_all', or a literal frame protocol_key (e.g. 'rsp_0xc0'). Walked left-to-right; the first scope with a populated slot wins. Defaults to ['protocol_all'] when omitted.

field_class.description: Semantic classification of the field. 'sensor': decode-only, no encode path. 'trigger': encode-only, fire-and-forget action with no read-back. 'stateful_bool': bidirectional boolean toggle. 'stateful_enum': bidirectional discrete-set selector. 'stateful_numeric': bidirectional continuous-numeric setpoint (e.g. humidity_setpoint in [0,100] %) — neither bool nor discrete enum, but a single scalar with a valid_range and step. 'complex_function': multi-key composite, may have state-machine semantics, may share wire bytes with siblings. 'unknown': inference could not assign a class; needs manual review. Optional — when omitted, downstream tools may fall back to inference from data_type and protocols. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 2 — field_class.

composite.description: For fields whose user-visible value spans multiple internal sub-fields (e.g. a setpoint composed of hour + step + minute sub-bytes). The members are internal-only; they exist for the codec's encode/decode walk and are not separately exported. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — composite.

derived_from.description: Receive-side multi-input formula that synthesises the user-visible value from already-decoded sibling members or other glossary fields. The 'inputs' list is the dependency declaration the codec uses to topologically sort pass-2 fields. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 1 — Foundational principle and Part 3 — derived_from.

decompose_to.description: Encode-side formula list — the inverse of derived_from. Each entry expands the user-visible value into one named sub-field via a single-output formula. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — decompose_to.

state_machine.description: For complex_function fields where transitions have side effects (e.g. setting one value forces sibling fields to fixed values). Declares the state space and the transitions between states; the codec consumes this generically. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — state_machine.

triggers.description: For trigger-class fields (fire-and-forget actions). Lists which sibling fields the trigger resets. The codec uses this to predict device-side state transitions after a trigger is sent. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — triggers.

shares_byte_with.description: Map of wire-byte position (e.g. 'bodyBytes[8]') to a list of sibling internal-key names that share the same byte. The encoder must reconstruct the byte from all listed siblings before writing, otherwise sibling values get clobbered. Discovered automatically by the field-extractor's shared-bytes pass. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — shares_byte_with.

ux.description: UX-layer metadata: mode visibility fencing, hardware capability gating, provenance. Consumed by the mode gate (StatusDB), command builder UX masking, and HA entity visibility.

ha.description: Home Assistant entity metadata: device_class, state_class, unit, precision, entity_category, enabled_default, off_behavior. Consumed by the HA integration's per-platform entity classes (sensor, binary_sensor, switch, select, number).

mutual_exclusion.description: Cross-field validity constraints: 'when this field is on, force these other fields to these values' (and inverse for when_off). Sourced from the gating analyzer's forced-state cascades. See serial_glossary_guide.md Part 3 — mutual_exclusion.

_migration.description: Audit-trail block written by the migration script. Records the inferred class, the inference reason, and (when populated) the previous feature_available value before any default-deny demotion. Free-form additionalProperties allowed so the migration script can extend without schema bumps.

range.description: [min, max] of the decoded value in physical units.

values.description: For enum/bitmask types: map of symbolic name → {raw, description}.

alt_names.description: Map of source project → field name used in that source.

protocols.description: Map of context key (e.g. serial_set, serial_response) → protocol location.

xye_note.description: How this field maps on the XYE bus (different encoding/byte position).

mainboard_note.description: How this field maps on the display↔mainboard bus.

ir_note.description: How this field maps in IR remote frames.

disambiguation.description: Explains naming conflicts or encoding asymmetries for this field.

note.description: Additional context not covered by other properties.

sources.description: Evidence sources: capture sessions, community projects, findings.

feature_available.description: Access level. 'always': read+write, no cap needed. 'readable': decode-only until cap upgrades to writable. 'capability': unavailable until B5 cap confirms. 'never': disabled (service/disputed).

power_independent.description: Whether this control can be written when the unit is powered off. Default false (requires power on). Only meaningful for control fields.

default_value.description: Protocol-reserved fixed value the encoder must write regardless of user input. Used for reserved/bit-pattern fields whose wire bits are fixed by the protocol spec (e.g. protocol_bit1 always 1, swing_reserved always 3). Reading from the glossary keeps the encoder generic: no field-name branches in Python. Only meaningful for settable fields; ignored on read-only/sensor entries.

read_value.description: Last value read/decoded from a frame. Null when not yet received. Populated by parsers at runtime, not by hand.

cap_id.description: B5 capability TLV record ID (hex), e.g. '0x10'.

cap_id_16.description: 16-bit capability ID including type prefix (dudanov format), e.g. '0x0210'.

capability.description: B5 capability describing valid values for this field. Frame-centric: lists which frames report it. Values carry constraints inline (the value IS the constraint). For data-carried caps, 'decode' describes per-byte extraction.

cap_type.description: TLV type: simple (0x00, boolean) or extended (0x02, value map).

frames.description: Frames that report this capability, keyed by frame identifier. Same frame-first pattern as field protocols.

values.description: For enum-mapped caps: map of value → {description, valid_range|valid_set, step, correction}. Each value carries the constraint it imposes.

decode.description: For data-carried caps: per-byte extraction rules. Each key names a decoded field (e.g. cool_min, cool_max).

constraints.description: Global validation rules that always apply regardless of capabilities (encoding limits, state requirements). Capability-specific constraints live inline in capability.values.

global.description: Rules that always apply (encoding limits, state preconditions).

cap_default.description: Pre-B5 constraints applied at boot for readable fields. Replaced by per-cap-value constraints once B5 arrives. Until the device's capability scan provides field-specific constraint data, readable fields must fall back to the most permissive constraint set the protocol allows — otherwise valid frames decoded before the first B5 reply would be rejected.

by_mode.description: Per-mode default constraints, same shape as the runtime active_constraints.by_mode dict.

cap_value.description: A single capability value with the constraint it imposes on the parent field.

feature_available.description: Overrides the field-level feature_available when this cap value is active.

encoding.description: Key into top-level encodings dict. Overrides the protocol entry's encoding when this cap value is active. Used when capability switches the decode format (e.g. BCD vs linear).

cap_decode_field.description: Byte-level decode rule for a data-carried capability.

offset.description: Byte offset within the capability data payload.

mode.description: Operating mode this decoded value applies to.

role.description: What this decoded value represents in the constraint.

correction_strategy.description: How to correct an out-of-range value. clamp: limit to min/max. snap_nearest: round to closest valid value. reject: refuse the command. ignore: silently drop the field.

constraint_rule.description: A single validation rule. In 'global': always applies. In 'by_capability': applies when the device reports the keyed cap value.

valid_range.description: [min, max] inclusive range of valid decoded values.

valid_set.description: Exhaustive list of valid values. Use instead of valid_range for discrete/enum fields.

step.description: Resolution step within the valid range, e.g. 0.5 for half-degree steps.

requires.description: State precondition, e.g. 'power == on'. If not met, the correction applies.

mode.description: Operating mode this rule applies to (cool, heat, auto, dry, fan). Omit for mode-independent rules.

composite_member_wire.description: Wire-byte location of a composite-member sub-field.

byte_position.description: Wire byte position string, e.g. 'bodyBytes[5]' or 'messageBytes[cursor + 3]'.

bit_range.description: [high_bit, low_bit] inclusive within the byte. Omit for full-byte members.

bit_range_TODO.description: True when the bit_range was inferred to exist but not yet hand-annotated. Skeleton marker; resolved during follow-up review.

composite_member.description: One internal sub-field that contributes to a composite field's user-visible value.

range.description: [min, max] of the sub-field in its raw integer units.

oneOf.description: List form when the sub-field appears at multiple wire positions (e.g. cmd vs response frames).

members.description: Map of sub-field name to its composite_member declaration.

formula.description: Sandboxed expression that synthesises the user-visible value from the listed inputs.

inputs.description: Names of fields or composite-member sub-fields the formula reads. Used by the codec's pass-2 topological sort.

target.description: Name of the sub-field this formula populates.

formula.description: Sandboxed expression that produces the sub-field's value from the user-visible 'value' input.

signature.description: Map of internal-key name to the value that defines this state.

observable.description: Whether the decoder may produce this state from the wire. Default true.

emittable.description: Whether the encoder may produce this state on writes. Default true. Setting false marks 'undefined' or 'inconsistent' states the decoder can observe but the encoder must never emit.

from.description: Source state name, or list of source state names if the same transition applies to several.

encoder_writes.description: Map of internal-key name to the value the encoder must write when applying this transition.

side_effects.description: Annotations describing destructive writes or other side effects the user should be warned about.

resets.description: Names of sibling fields that the trigger resets when fired.

shares_byte_with.description: Map of wire byte position string to the list of sibling internal-key names that share that byte.

ux_block.description: UX-layer metadata controlling field visibility and gating.

visible_in_modes.description: Operating modes in which this field is visible and writable. Used by the StatusDB mode gate (write fence) and command builder UX mask (frame-level zeroing). Mode names match operating_mode enum labels: auto, cool, dry, heat, fan, smart_dry.

hardware_flag.description: B5 capability flag name that must be truthy for the field to be visible. When the flag is absent or falsy, the field is permanently masked.

sources.description: Provenance note: where the UX constraint was derived from (e.g. 'own observation', 'community protocol research').

note.description: Additional context about the UX constraint — edge cases, exceptions, device-family differences.

forces.description: Map of sibling field name to forced value.

migration_block.description: Audit-trail block written by the migration script. Free-form so the script can extend without schema bumps.

ha_block.description: Home Assistant entity metadata. All keys optional; consumed by the HA integration when present.

state_class.description: HA SensorStateClass. 'total_increasing' is the Energy Dashboard contract for cumulative counters.

unit_of_measurement.description: HA-facing display unit. May differ from the protocol `unit` field.

suggested_display_precision.description: Number of decimal digits to show in the UI.

entity_category.description: HA entity category; places the entity under Configuration or Diagnostics.

enabled_default.description: When false, the entity is created but disabled by default in the HA registry.

off_behavior.description: Read policy when AC power=off. 'hide' masks the value to None (default); 'available' returns the device's reported value unchanged — used for always-valid thermistors, error codes, and cumulative counters.

category_group.description: A subcategory grouping containing field definitions.
